chapter_title.ar: باب الْوَقْتِ الَّذِي تَبِينُ مِنْهُ الْمُطَلَّقَةُ وَ الَّذِي يَكُونُ فِيهِ الرَّجْعَةُ مَتَى يَجُوزُ لَهَا أَنْ تَتَزَوَّجَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 26 – The time which she becomes irrevocably divorced from him, and in which there happens to be the reversion, when is it allowed for her to re-marry?

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (21/150)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (21/150)
text: صَفْوَانُ عَنِ ابْنِ بُكَيْرٍ عَنْ زُرَارَةَ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ الْمُطَلَّقَةُ تَبِينُ عِنْدَ أَوَّلِ قَطْرَةٍ مِنَ الدَّمِ فِي الْقُرْءِ الْأَخِيرِ .

en.hubeali: Humeyd Bin Ziyad, from Ibn Sama’at, from Safwan, from Ibn Muskan, from Zurara – similar to it’.
Safwan, from Ibn Bukeyr, from Zurara,from Abu Ja’farasws, said,
‘I heard him<sup>asws</sup> saying: ‘The divorced woman is irrevocably divorced during the first drop of the blood in the last menstruation’.
en.sarwar: 7. Safwan has narrated from ibn Bukayr from Zurarah who has said the foliowing: “I once heard abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, saying, ‘A woman divorced becomes stranger to him with the first drop of blood because of Hayd (menses) during the last Hayd (menses)-free period.’”
